title: Dental Care for Veterans Act
plain_summary: This bill changes the rules so that all veterans who get health care from the VA can also get dental care from the VA. Right now, only some veterans can get dental care through the VA if they have teeth problems caused by their military service or meet other special requirements. The new dental care will start slowly over four years, with veterans who need it most getting it first. Veterans with disabilities from their service, former prisoners of war, and those who got Purple Hearts will be among the first to get dental care.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Dental Care for Veterans Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Requirement That Secretary of Veterans Affairs Furnish Dental Care in the Same Manner as Any Other Medical Service
sections.text: This section makes changes to how the Department of Veterans Affairs provides dental care to veterans. The law removes special restrictions on dental care and makes it available like any other medical service. The Secretary of Veterans Affairs can provide dentures and other dental appliances to veterans. The changes will happen in phases over four years. Veterans who already get dental care from the VA will continue getting it right away when this law takes effect. Other veterans will become eligible for dental care based on their priority group. Veterans in the highest priority groups will get dental care one year after this law passes. Veterans in lower priority groups will wait two, three, or four years depending on their group. The law also makes technical changes to update references in other parts of the veterans' code to match these new rules.
